diagrams.title: File Attributes Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the components of file metadata stored in FCB/inode
diagrams.purpose: Understanding what information OS maintains about each file

diagrams.title: Sequential vs Direct vs Indexed Access
diagrams.description: Three different methods of accessing records in a file
elements: Direct: Arrow jumping from 'base' directly to Record N
Indexed: Index file → pointers → Data blocks (two-step)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how different access methods retrieve file records

diagrams.title: File Operations Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the lifecycle of file operations from creation to deletion
elements: FCB loaded at OPEN, updated at each R/W, written back at CLOSE
File descriptor allocated at OPEN, released at CLOSE
diagrams.purpose: Understanding state changes and resource management in file handling

diagrams.title: Hard Link vs Symbolic Link
diagrams.description: Comparison of two types of links in Unix file systems
elements: Hard Link: Two directory entries → Same Inode → Same Data Blocks
Symbolic Link: Directory entry → Special file (contains path) → Target Inode → Data Blocks
Shows link count incrementing for hard links
